Politics.headline: Trump Files Emergency Appeal to Keep Building White House Ballroom
Politics.detail: Justice Department lawyers stated that pausing construction puts the president at risk, as Trump's team files an emergency appeal to continue building a White House ballroom. President Trump has been pushing for the ballroom's construction, displaying renderings of the proposed project.

Politics.headline: US aviator rescued as Trump vows strikes on Iran's infrastructure if Strait of Hormuz isn't reopened
Politics.detail: Following the downing of a U.S. warplane, a U.S. pilot was rescued from Iran, prompting President Trump to threaten strikes on Iranian infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened. Tensions remain high in the region.

Politics.headline: Trump Uses Expletive-Ridden Social Media Post to Threaten Iran's Infrastructure
Politics.detail: President Trump used abusive language in a social media post threatening Iranian infrastructure and demanding that Iran open the Strait of Hormuz or face severe consequences. This follows a series of escalating events in the region.

Politics.headline: Giuffre family urges UK's King Charles to meet Epstein survivors in US
Politics.detail: The Giuffre family is publicly calling for King Charles to meet with survivors of Jeffrey Epstein's abuse in the United States. This request comes amid renewed attention to the Epstein case.

Politics.headline: US revokes green cards and visas of several Iranian nationals connected to Tehran government
Politics.detail: The United States has revoked the green cards and visas of several Iranian nationals believed to have connections to the government in Tehran. This action is part of the ongoing tensions between the two countries.

Finance & Economy.headline: Higher energy costs from Iran war could threaten fragile economics of AI boom
Finance & Economy.detail: Market volatility caused by the Middle East conflict exposes energy traders to heavy losses and rumors of insider trading. Higher energy costs stemming from the Iran war could pose a threat to the already delicate economics of the burgeoning AI sector.

Finance & Economy.headline: Stocks recover from early losses and close with a weekly gain. US oil tops $110 a barrel
Finance & Economy.detail: After experiencing initial losses, stocks rebounded to close with a weekly gain, while the price of US oil surpassed $110 per barrel. Financial markets are experiencing volatility due to the ongoing war in Iran.

Finance & Economy.headline: CII's 20-Point Policy Agenda calls for coordinated fiscal, financial and trade response to West Asia crisis
Finance & Economy.detail: The Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) has proposed a 20-point policy agenda that advocates for a coordinated fiscal, financial, and trade response to the ongoing crisis in West Asia. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is expected to maintain stable rates in its first monetary policy announcement since the onset of the West Asia conflict, according to an SBI report.

Finance & Economy.headline: 3 ways the Iran war is hitting Americans' pocketbooks
Finance & Economy.detail: U.S. consumers are starting to feel the financial impact of the Iran war. Mounting inflation risks complicate the picture on interest rates, according to Goolsbee, president of the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago.

Finance & Economy.headline: Canada needs to hike GST and cut non-defence spending to meet 5% NATO target: report
Finance & Economy.detail: A recent report suggests that Canada should increase its Goods and Services Tax (GST) and reduce non-defence expenditures to meet its commitment of allocating 5% of its GDP to NATO. Canada's trade deficit has widened due to increased gold imports.

Technology.headline: AI Can Now Prescribe You Psychiatric Medication in Utah
Technology.detail: A new development in Utah allows AI to prescribe psychiatric medication, raising questions about the future of mental healthcare and the role of technology in medical decisions.

Technology.headline: Copilot Terms Claim Microsoft's AI Is for 'Entertainment Purposes Only'
Technology.detail: Microsoft's Copilot terms state the AI is for entertainment, sparking debate about the reliability and responsibility associated with AI-driven tools in practical applications.

Technology.headline: Lenovo's Legion Go 2 Is the Latest Console to Raise Prices
Technology.detail: Lenovo increases the price of its Legion Go 2 console, following a trend in the gaming industry, impacting consumers and market competitiveness.

Technology.headline: Windows 11 Abandoned Native Apps. Now It Needs Them to Survive.
Technology.detail: The article discusses how Windows 11's shift away from native apps is creating challenges for its long-term viability, emphasizing the importance of these apps for the operating system's functionality and user experience.

Technology.headline: AI Glasses Shift Into Momentum Mode, Shipments Grow 322% in 2025
Technology.detail: Shipments of AI glasses surged by 322% in 2025, reaching 8.7 million units, driven by advancements in multimodal AI and lighter designs, according to a recent report.

Science.headline: Hidden Roman Sanctuary Discovered Beneath Frankfurt
Science.detail: A hidden Roman sanctuary discovered beneath Frankfurt is offering rare clues about ancient rituals, including possible human sacrifice. With major funding secured, scientists are now racing to uncover its secrets.

Science.headline: Single Protein Linked to Brain Aging and Memory Decline
Science.detail: Scientists have uncovered a powerful new clue in the mystery of brain aging: a single protein called FTL1. In aging mice, higher levels of this protein weakened connections between brain cells and led to memory decline.

Science.headline: Nanoscale Structure Traps Infrared Light
Science.detail: Researchers have created a nanoscale structure that traps infrared light in a layer just 40 nanometers thick—over 1,000 times thinner than a human hair. By using a unique material with exceptional light-bending properties, they can confine and manipulate light at this scale.

Science.headline: Mars Buzzing with Hidden Electrical Activity
Science.detail: Mars may look like a quiet, dusty world, but it's actually buzzing with hidden electrical activity. Powerful dust storms and swirling dust devils generate static electricity strong enough to spark and influence the Martian atmosphere.

Science.headline: Artificial Saliva Protects Teeth and Fights Bacteria
Science.detail: Scientists have created an artificial saliva using a sugarcane protein that can protect teeth and fight bacteria. The key ingredient, CANECPI-5, binds directly to enamel, forming a shield against harmful microorganisms and decay.

Health.headline: AI Drug Discovery Platform Launched to Tackle Global Diseases
Health.detail: A new AI platform, dd4gh (Drug Design for Global Health), has been launched to accelerate the development of treatments for major global diseases like malaria and tuberculosis, offering free access to eligible researchers worldwide, integrating predictive and generative AI to analyze datasets and identify drug candidates efficiently. As global health systems face increasing pressure, such innovations may play a critical role in speeding up the delivery of life-saving therapies to the populations that need them most.

Health.headline: JU PHI Department to observe World Health Day with daylong programmes
Health.detail: The Public Health Forum of the Public Health and Informatics (PHI) Department at Jahangirnagar University will observe World Health Day-2026 on April 7 with a series of day-long programmes aimed at promoting health awareness and collective responsibility. This year's theme, “Together for Health, Stand with Science,” highlights the importance of collective action in addressing global health challenges and emphasizes reliance on scientific evidence and data-driven approaches.

Health.headline: Ontario misses federal funding deadline for nurse practitioners
Health.detail: Ontario has missed the federal funding deadline for nurse practitioners. No further details provided in the immediate context.

Health.headline: Dangerous bacterial infection hits highest level seen in Canada in more than a decade.
Health.detail: Annual cases of invasive meningococcal disease, a rare but dangerous bacterial infection, have hit the highest level seen in Canada in more than a decade. This rapidly-progressing illness can cause meningitis, sepsis or even death, and children and young adults often bear the brunt.

Health.headline: Massachusetts man speaks again after larynx transplant
Health.detail: A Massachusetts man can speak again after surgeons removed his cancerous voice box and replaced it with a donated one, a pioneering move. Marty Kedian is only the third patient in the U.S. ever to undergo a larynx transplant — the first with active cancer — and one of a handful worldwide.

Sports.headline: Jokic scores 40, outduels Wembanyama and Nuggets top Spurs 136-134 in OT
Sports.detail: Nikola Jokic scored 40 points and the Denver Nuggets defeated the San Antonio Spurs 136-134 in overtime.

Sports.headline: Stephen Curry set to return for Warriors against Rockets after two-month absence with knee injury
Sports.detail: Stephen Curry is set to make his return for the Golden State Warriors against the Houston Rockets after being sidelined for two months due to a knee injury.

Sports.headline: Michigan rout Arizona to set up final against UConn
Sports.detail: Michigan dominated Arizona in the NCAA Tournament, securing a spot in the final against UConn.

Sports.headline: IPL 2026 Live: Focus On Virat Kohli, Sanju Samson As RCB, CSK Face Off
Sports.detail: The focus is on Virat Kohli and Sanju Samson as RCB and CSK face off in the IPL 2026 live match.

Sports.headline: Sabres clinch playoff spot, end historic postseason drought
Sports.detail: The Buffalo Sabres have clinched a playoff spot, ending their historic postseason drought.

Entertainment.headline: Chetak Screen Awards 2026 LIVE Updates: India's credible film awards back; Dhurandhar leads with 24 nominations
Entertainment.detail: The Chetak Screen Awards 2026 are celebrating cinematic excellence across 31 categories on a live broadcast for the first time in 20 years, with 'Dhurandhar' leading the nominations.

Entertainment.headline: Box Office Report [April 4, 2026]: Dhurandhar 2 witnesses rise on Saturday | See total collection so far
Entertainment.detail: Dhurandhar 2, directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, has become the highest-grossing Indian film, surpassing its prequel's record, with worldwide collections aiming to match 'Pushpa 2'.

Entertainment.headline: Paresh Rawal praises Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar says 'I felt I should have been part of it'
Entertainment.detail: Paresh Rawal expressed his admiration for Ranveer Singh's film 'Dhurandhar', regretting that he wasn't involved in the project.

Entertainment.headline: TWICE reflect on a decade as a group: 'There's so much more we can do' | AP interview
Entertainment.detail: TWICE members Nayeon, Jeongyeon, Momo, Sana, Jihyo, Mina, Dahyun, Chaeyoung, and Tzuyu discuss their anniversary release, solo tracks, evolving definitions of success, and creative process in an AP interview.

Entertainment.headline: Damon Albarn and Jamie Hewlett on 'The Mountain' and building the Gorillaz | AP interview
Entertainment.detail: Damon Albarn and Jamie Hewlett discuss their creative process, the virtual band's return, and the mythology of the Gorillaz animated universe in an AP interview about their new album 'The Mountain'.

Crime & Justice.headline: Patient stabbed in Edmonton ER waiting room as dozens witness attack
Crime & Justice.detail: A patient was stabbed in the waiting room of an Edmonton emergency room while dozens of people witnessed the attack; police are investigating.

Crime & Justice.headline: Two dead in late-night shooting at Brampton elementary school, police searching for suspects
Crime & Justice.detail: Police are investigating a late-night shooting at an elementary school in Brampton that left two people dead, and they are currently searching for suspects.

Crime & Justice.headline: Tiger Woods released on bail hours after arrest on suspicion of DUI
Crime & Justice.detail: Tiger Woods was released on bail hours after being arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man banned from Planet Fitness over unpaid bill returns to gym to stab employee: Police
Crime & Justice.detail: A man who was banned from Planet Fitness for an unpaid bill returned to the gym and stabbed an employee, according to police reports.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man killed his own mother with a knife in their apartment and then called 911 on himself
Crime & Justice.detail: A man killed his mother with a knife in their apartment and then called 911 to report the crime.

Education.headline: Harvard Announces 6 Free Online Courses In AI, Programming For Learners, Application Link Here
Education.detail: Harvard University has announced six free online courses in AI and programming, providing accessible learning opportunities for individuals interested in these fields.

Education.headline: UGC NET December 2025: NTA Releases Revised Final Answer Key, Drops Questions
Education.detail: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has released the revised final answer key for the UGC NET December 2025 exam, and has dropped some questions in History and Commerce.

Education.headline: Ethiopia's largest university partners with regional bureaus to enhance Chinese language education
Education.detail: Addis Ababa University (AAU) has signed a cooperation agreement with the Addis Ababa and Oromia education bureaus to improve Chinese language education in secondary schools, focusing on teacher training and certification.

Education.headline: Palestinian Children's Day marked by rising toll, widespread disruption to education
Education.detail: Palestinian Children's Day is observed amid concerns about the impact of conflict on children in Gaza and the West Bank, including casualties, school damage, and humanitarian challenges; more than 19,000 children have been killed since the start of the conflict in Gaza on October 7, 2023.

Education.headline: Leading the Change: CSCU Embraces Innovation, Preparing Students for Jobs of Tomorrow
Education.detail: Connecticut State Colleges and Universities (CSCU) are designing programs and learning environments to prepare students for the future of work through strategic partnerships and integration of emerging technologies.

Lifestyle.headline: Mentalist Oz Pearlman to headline 2026 White House Correspondents' Dinner
Lifestyle.detail: Mentalist Oz Pearlman is set to headline the 2026 White House Correspondents' Dinner, promising an evening of mind-bending entertainment.

Lifestyle.headline: Dinosaur chicken nuggets sold nationwide at Walmart may contain lead, federal alert warns
Lifestyle.detail: A federal alert has been issued warning that dinosaur-shaped chicken nuggets sold at Walmart stores across the nation may contain lead.

Lifestyle.headline: Comeuppance: how an orgasmic 'cult' ended in a prison term for its founder
Lifestyle.detail: An orgasmic 'cult' that combined spirituality, mindfulness, and sexuality ended with its founder facing controversy and a prison sentence.

Lifestyle.headline: This is how we do it: 'The fact he's comfortable enough with his sexuality to be intimate with other men is so hot to me'
Lifestyle.detail: An article discussing intimacy and sexuality, highlighting the appeal of a partner being comfortable with their sexuality and intimate with other men.

Lifestyle.headline: Chesney the kangaroo scales tall fence and flees petting zoo for three days on the lam
Lifestyle.detail: Chesney, a kangaroo, escaped from a petting zoo by scaling a tall fence and remained on the run for three days.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: During dark times on Earth, space provides a source of light
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Pittsburgh Post-Gazette highlights the Artemis program as a symbol of hope and a new beginning for humanity amidst global turmoil and the potential end of the post-World War II global system. It suggests that the habits needed for space exploration can also build better political communities on Earth.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Maine businesses are right to resist data privacy bill
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial argues against a data privacy bill in Maine that would limit businesses' ability to advertise online, suggesting it goes too far and puts them at a competitive disadvantage. The piece notes an exemption for political organizations highlights the bill's negative impacts.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion | Taiwan's self-defense paradox
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Washington Post discusses the challenges Taiwan faces in balancing its relationship with China and the United States, emphasizing the need for a credible military deterrent to forestall potential aggression from mainland China. The editorial highlights the difficulty of reaching a peaceful political settlement that upholds Taiwan's autonomy and freedoms.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Veterans spending has surged. Are veterans better off?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Washington Post examines the significant increase in spending on Veterans Affairs, questioning whether the outcomes for veterans have improved proportionally. Despite a 446% increase in spending between 2004 and 2024, the editorial points to issues such as high rates of alcohol and drug use among younger veterans and a shortage of mental health care providers.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Carman: War and hostility toward renewables deliver huge profits for Trump's big funders
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Colorado Sun discusses how the oil and gas industry has benefited from tax incentives and the war in Iran, leading to increased profit forecasts for volatile oil industry stocks. The author criticizes the manipulation of the energy market and the irrational protections for fossil fuel technologies.

World.headline: US Rescues Second Crew Member of Downed F-15E Fighter Jet From Iran
World.detail: A U.S. crew member who had been missing since Iran shot down a fighter plane has been rescued, according to President Trump.

World.headline: Ukraine Hits Port in Russia's Primorsk, Oil Refinery in Nizhny Novgorod
World.detail: Ukraine has struck a port in Russia's Primorsk and an oil refinery in Nizhny Novgorod, causing damage.

World.headline: Kuwait's Power, Water Plants Damaged as Iran Keeps Attacking Gulf States
World.detail: Kuwait's power and water plants have been damaged amidst ongoing attacks in the Gulf, with Bahrain and the UAE also reporting attacks that resulted in fires.

World.headline: One Killed and Buildings Damaged as Magnitude 7.4 Earthquake Strikes Indonesia
World.detail: A magnitude 7.4 earthquake struck Indonesia, resulting in one death and damage to buildings.

World.headline: Israel's Parliament Approves Death Penalty for Palestinians Convicted of Murdering Israelis
World.detail: The Israeli parliament has approved the death penalty for Palestinians who are convicted of murdering Israelis.

Zimbabwe.headline: Man jailed six years for killing wife in drunken row
Zimbabwe.detail: A man was sentenced to six years in prison for killing his wife during a drunken argument.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chief Dakamela of Nkayi gets virgin wife as present from South African royal family
Zimbabwe.detail: Chief Dakamela of Nkayi received a virgin wife as a gift from a South African royal family.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwean bakery manager dismissed for posting bible verse on WhatsApp group
Zimbabwe.detail: A Zimbabwean bakery manager was fired for sharing a bible verse in a WhatsApp group.

Zimbabwe.headline: Heritage School to host elite academies' U21 league, as season kicks off
Zimbabwe.detail: Heritage School will host the U21 league for elite academies as the season commences.

Zimbabwe.headline: Warriors set to participate at Unity Cup in the United Kingdom
Zimbabwe.detail: The Warriors are preparing to participate in the Unity Cup in the United Kingdom.
